input.headline: The Nazis on trial: The enduring lessons of Nuremberg, 80 years on

raw.headline: The Nazis on trial: The enduring lessons of Nuremberg, 80 years on

winner: At the Nuremberg trials, they had a special rule: no swastika armbands allowed in court. One defendant got caught sneaking one in—turns out he was just trying to mark his spot like everyone else at the buffet.

candidates.text: The Nuremberg trials taught us that justice delayed is justice denied—but apparently, the Nazis forgot to check their calendar.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on a historical reference with concise, specific humor."}

candidates.text: The Nazis on trial for "not paying their Nazi taxes" — turns out, they had a Nuremberg defense.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'Nuremberg defense' with a twist of tax humor; concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: The judge asked the defendant why he joined the Nazi party, and he replied, "Well, I was told it was a great place to learn how to organize a really efficient lunch rush.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific with the lunch rush reference, but it's slightly generic in its approach and lacks deeper humor or punch."}

candidates.text: After eighty years of legal review, it seems the Nuremberg trials were really the first time anyone successfully put the "pro" in "prosecution.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'prosecution' with historical context, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: At the Nuremberg trials, they had a special rule: no swastika armbands allowed in court. One defendant got caught sneaking one in—turns out he was just trying to mark his spot like everyone else at the buffet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor feels forced and risks being insensitive given the subject matter. The comparison to a buffet is generic and lacks the sharpness needed for a strong punchline."}

candidates.text: The Nazis are being put on trial for the crime of being on trial for eighty years, which is a very long time to spend in a courtroom without a snack break.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the context, but it's a bit generic in its approach and lacks deeper wit. The humor relies on a play on 'being on trial for eighty years,' which is clever but not particularly original or sharp."}

candidates.text: After eighty years of reflection, the court finally reached a verdict on the Nuremberg trials: the Nazis were found guilty of being the first people in history to manage to make "the ban on everything" a full-time job.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever and specific, using a modern twist on historical events. It's somewhat surprising and concise, though the humor relies on a niche reference that may not resonate with all audiences."}

candidates.text: At Nuremberg, they said, 'Never forget.' Well, we didn’t—we just forgot which side was ours!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the context of Nuremberg, but it risks being offensive due to its casual handling of a sensitive historical topic. It also lacks full clarity in its humor."}

candidates.text: Hitler’s trial was so unfair—it even had a "no snacks" policy!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 4.5, "reason": "The joke is offensive and trivializes a serious historical event."}
